questions.question: What is a dharmachakra?
questions.correct_answer: A Buddhist wheel of law, also known as a dhamma chakra
questions.explanation: A symbol of Buddhism, representing the teachings of Buddha

questions.question: Who is a Pradakshina?
questions.correct_answer: A person who has attained enlightenment
questions.explanation: An enlightened being, often referred to as a Buddha

questions.question: What is a Darwani?
questions.explanation: One of the seven sacred rivers in Hinduism

questions.question: Who is Raghuputra?
questions.correct_answer: A term for Lord Rama, the seventh avatar of Vishnu
questions.explanation: A title given to Lord Rama, a central figure in Hindu mythology

questions.question: What is Malaya Pavan?
questions.correct_answer: The wind from the Malaya mountains
questions.explanation: A term used to describe the gentle breeze from the Western Ghats

questions.question: Who is Shri Krishna?
questions.correct_answer: An avatar of Lord Vishnu, a central figure in Hindu mythology
questions.explanation: The eighth avatar of Lord Vishnu, often referred to as a divine prince

questions.question: What is the Bhagavad Gita?
questions.correct_answer: A sacred Hindu scripture and part of the Mahabharata
questions.explanation: A dialogue between Prince Arjuna and Lord Krishna, discussing spiritual principles

questions.question: Who is Jan Gan Man?
questions.explanation: A song written by Rabindranath Tagore, adopted as the national anthem in 1950

questions.question: What is the importance of the concept of Matrudevobhava?
questions.correct_answer: It represents the reverence for and respect towards one's mother
questions.explanation: A phrase used to express love, devotion, and respect towards one's mother

questions.question: Who wrote Vande Mataram?
questions.explanation: An Indian poet and writer, who wrote the original Bengali version of the song

important_notes.title: The concept of Dharma
important_notes.content: Dharma refers to one's duty or moral obligations towards oneself and others. It is a fundamental principle in Hinduism and Buddhism.

important_notes.title: Importance of Bhakti
important_notes.content: Bhakti, or devotion, is an essential aspect of Hinduism, emphasizing the importance of loving and revering a higher power

important_notes.title: The significance of the Ganges River
important_notes.content: The Ganges River holds great spiritual significance in Hinduism, representing purification and rebirth

important_notes.title: Understanding Prana
important_notes.content: Prana is the vital energy that flows through all living beings, believed to be connected to the breath

important_notes.title: Importance of Yajna
important_notes.content: Yajna, or sacrifice, holds great importance in Hinduism and Buddhism, symbolizing selfless giving and devotion

important_notes.title: Understanding Karma
important_notes.content: Karma refers to the universal law of cause and effect, where an individual's actions determine their future

important_notes.title: The significance of Mantra
important_notes.content: Mantras are sacred sounds or phrases used in meditation and spiritual practices, believed to possess spiritual power

important_notes.title: Importance of Yoga
important_notes.content: Yoga is a physical, mental, and spiritual practice that aims to balance and harmonize the body, mind, and spirit

important_notes.title: Understanding Reincarnation
important_notes.content: Reincarnation refers to the cycle of birth, death, and rebirth in Hinduism and Buddhism, governed by karma

important_notes.title: The concept of Ahimsa
important_notes.content: Ahimsa is a fundamental principle in Hinduism and Buddhism, emphasizing compassion, non-violence, and harmlessness towards all living beings

facts.fact: The Bhagavad Gita is an integral part of the Mahabharata epic
Rabindranath Tagore wrote the national anthem of India, Jan Gan Man
Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay was a Bengali poet and writer who wrote Vande Mataram
The Ganges River is considered one of the seven sacred rivers in Hinduism
Prana, or vital energy, is believed to flow through all living beings
Yajna, or sacrifice, symbolizes selfless giving and devotion in Hinduism and Buddhism
Mantras are sacred sounds or phrases used in meditation and spiritual practices
Yoga is a physical, mental, and spiritual practice aimed at balancing and harmonizing the body, mind, and spirit
Reincarnation refers to the cycle of birth, death, and rebirth governed by karma in Hinduism and Buddhism
Ahimsa is a fundamental principle emphasizing compassion, non-violence, and harmlessness towards all living beings
